title: New Theory NME is a Remake
display_title: New Theory NME is a Remake
wikitext:
[]: New Theory: NME is a Remake             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Aug 20, 2010 5:52 pm So we have a lot of speculation that NME might be a sequel, a prequel, and now even a midquel, but what if-- and this might seem crazy, but bear with me for a moment-- it's actually a completely separate continuity? Now before you start typing up an angry response debunking my hypothesis or accusing me of trolling, allow me to explain. I know Ootsuki said in that Newtype thing from 2007 that Rebuild was not a remake, but a "new work", but a lot has changed since then. According to Sadamoto, they've gone in a different direction from what was planned at the start. So couldn't NME conceivably turn out to be a remake? Evidence: certain things have changed, like Asuka's surname, the addition of Mari, the Angel numbering, etc., but overall it's a pretty similar story that looks to be leading to a different conclusion. When it comes down to it, isn't that what remakes really are?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 20, 2010 6:05 pm I already said that in my thread that it IS most likely a remake and I just hope Q/Final won't suck. Well played. Well played indeed.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Aug 20, 2010 6:19 pm This theory is so crazy, it just might work.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Aug 20, 2010 7:31 pm Crazy shit. Mind=blown.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Aug 20, 2010 7:37 pm very possible indeed but it would explain the dislike of the films at this point, since "remakes" aren't regularly welcomed these days. again, I don't mind that either way (unless its something i've been very attached to since day 1 and is remade..into something that is very much pure shit. EVA, as much as I like it...is something that i've only been with since last year, so no offense.)

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Aug 20, 2010 7:40 pm God hath spoken! Inscribe it ye hymnals and ye prayerbooks!

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Fri Aug 20, 2010 8:12 pm But remakes suck!

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Aug 20, 2010 8:17 pm My reaction to your theory (but more male): (Also points to my sig vvv)

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Fri Aug 20, 2010 8:28 pm Excellent thread, NAveryW.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Aug 20, 2010 8:41 pm Without a doubt. Anything else is illogical.

[]: Re: New Theory: NME is a Remake             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Aug 20, 2010 10:29 pm NAveryW wrote: what if-- and this might seem crazy, but bear with me for a moment-- it's actually a completely separate continuity?

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Fri Aug 20, 2010 10:47 pm GasmaskAvenger wrote: very possible indeed but it would explain the dislike of the films at this point, since "remakes" aren't regularly welcomed these days. again, I don't mind that either way (unless its something i've been very attached to since day 1 and is remade..into something that is very much pure shit. EVA, as much as I like it...is something that i've only been with since last year, so no offense.)

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Aug 20, 2010 11:07 pm I was talking in references to them internet folk the mass public, for the most part, doesn't mind remakes (especially Hollywood ones)

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Sat Aug 21, 2010 12:23 am GasmaskAvenger wrote: I was talking in references to them internet folk the mass public, for the most part, doesn't mind remakes (especially Hollywood ones)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Aug 21, 2010 1:25 am People really don't need to like remakes, they just need to pay for them. And that means good business for Hollywood. I mean, you're still gonna go see Spider-Man 4 and Rebuild 3.0 , right? Even if it is just to go on the internet and complain about how they're not as good as the original.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sat Aug 21, 2010 1:41 am I see this thread taking a long, dark path to oblivion.....

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Sat Aug 21, 2010 2:20 am Wow. It's like findidg out what 'Redrum' is spelled backwards for the first time. Anyway, the simplest explination, right? Though not as entertaining when you just accept it. In all honesty, though, it's a loose/loose senerio here. It it's anything but a remake people will complain, if not people will complain. It doesn't matter how it all turns out you just can't please the fans.

[]: Re: New Theory: NME is a Remake             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Aug 21, 2010 2:42 am NAveryW wrote: So we have a lot of speculation that NME might be a sequel, a prequel, and now even a midquel, but what if-- and this might seem crazy, but bear with me for a moment-- it's actually a completely separate continuity?

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Sat Aug 21, 2010 4:30 am I mean, you're still gonna go see Spider-Man 4 and Rebuild 3.0, right? Even if it is just to go on the internet and complain about how they're not as good as the original.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Sat Aug 21, 2010 11:41 am NNAavveryWwwwwWww-kunnn , I have a theory that will destroy your theory and hijack this entire thread. If we go below 100 miles per hour this entire thing will fucking explode . You know how in the early parts of both NGE and Rebuild we see Rei appearing on the street for no reason? That's because she exists in all spaces and times at once . That means that both NGE and Rebuild are remakes of each other, or remakes of a later incarnation that only Rei and Kaworu know about. Well, it isn't later to them, but very much the present. It could only be later to Rei in the sense that she reaches something akin to godhood, but that could simply be one branch of a tree she's already grown, cut down, and turned into furniture. Furniture Kaworu has already sat upon . Do you see where I'm going with this? What I'm saying is that if you hook up two DVD players to the same TV and play both NGE and Rebuild in tandem, switching back and forth every 120 seconds, you'll experience a true epiphany. As Buddha said, the difference between nirvana and confusion is two pounds of opium and a shallow stream.
